title: If 2
description: You can chain multiple conditions with `else if` in Rust.
instructions: Complete the function to return the correct string for the given number using multiple conditions.
hints: Both branches of an if/else must return the same type.
The function returns &str, but the else branch returns an integer.
Add an `else if` for "fuzz" and fix the return values to be string slices.
explanation: All branches of an if/else expression must return the same type. Rust's type system enforces this at compile time. You can chain conditions with `else if` to handle multiple cases. Each branch must evaluate to a value of the declared return type.
